prompt: Eye-level interior of a small finished Andover MA hall bathroom. Walk-in shower stall on the right clad in classic 3x6 white subway tile with a thin black grout line, a niche shelf, brushed-nickel fixtures. A simple painted shaker vanity in soft sage green with a white quartz counter and an oil-rubbed bronze gooseneck faucet. Herringbone-pattern marble-look porcelain floor. A round black-framed mirror over the vanity. Shot with a 35mm prime lens, soft morning window light from a single small frosted window — even, calm, slightly cool. A small red ceramic toothbrush cup sits on the counter as the lone saturated color. Slightly desaturated, high-contrast, working-contractor catalog feel, NOT HGTV bright. No readable text, no logos.
